task_completion.description: Log in to Rocket.Chat as admin
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Rocket.Chat main interface is visible, indicating a successful login past the initial login screen.
The Administration panel or Workspace Settings menu is open in the Rocket.Chat interface.

task_completion.description: Navigate to the Message settings section
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Message' settings page is visible in the main content area.
The toggle or checkbox for 'Allow Message Editing' is set to the enabled/true state.

task_completion.description: Restrict the editing window to 2 minutes
task_completion.visual_evidence: The input field for 'Block Edit In Minutes' (or similar editing window setting) contains the value '2'.
The toggle or checkbox for 'Keep Editing History' is set to the enabled/true state.

integrity.description: The agent used the graphical user interface to make changes.
integrity.visual_evidence: Settings are changed via the Rocket.Chat web interface in a browser, not through terminal commands, API scripts (e.g., curl), or database modifications.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual Rocket.Chat application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show a real web browser rendering the Rocket.Chat application, not a text editor or mock interface.

integrity.description: The agent saved the configuration changes.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 'Save changes' button is clicked (often indicated by a loading state, the button becoming disabled after clicking, or a success toast notification appearing).

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target values to verify in the Message settings: 'Allow Message Editing' must be enabled (true), 'Block Edit In Minutes' must be exactly 2, and 'Keep Editing History' must be enabled (true).
